context: This chapter describes the rituals performed on the eleventh day after death. It covers the Vrishotsarga (release of a bull), the feeding of brahmins, specific offerings and donations made on this day, and their significance in aiding the departed soul's journey toward liberation.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
एकादशदिने कार्यं किमुद्दिश्य जनार्दन ।
वृषोत्सर्गादिकं कर्म तद्वदस्व ममाग्रतः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuda uvācha —
ekādaśa-dine kāryaṃ kimuddiśya janārdana |
vṛṣotsargādikaṃ karma tadvadas-va mamāgrataḥ ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: O Janardana, what should be done on the eleventh day, and with what purpose? Please describe to me the ceremony of the bull release and other rites.

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
एकादशेऽहनि प्राप्ते शुद्धः स्नातः समाहितः ।
ब्राह्मणान् भोजयेत् पूर्वं ततः कर्म समाचरेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvācha —
ekādaśe'hani prāpte śuddhaḥ snātaḥ samāhitaḥ |
brāhmaṇān bhojayet pūrvaṃ tataḥ karma samācharet ||
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: When the eleventh day arrives, having become purified, bathed, and focused, one should first feed the brahmins and then perform the rites.

verses.hindi:
शय्यादानं प्रकुर्वीत एकादशदिने नरः ।
सोपस्करां सवस्त्रां च सर्वोपकरणान्विताम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
śayyā-dānaṃ prakurvīta ekādaśa-dine naraḥ |
sopaskarāṃ savastrāṃ ca sarvopkaraṇānvitām ||
verses.translation: On the eleventh day, a person should make the gift of a bed, complete with its accessories, coverings, and all furnishings.

verses.hindi:
पादुके छत्रमासन्दीं ताम्बूलं भाजनानि च ।
ब्राह्मणाय प्रदद्यात् तु यद्यत् प्रेतस्य वाञ्छितम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
pāduke chatram āsandīṃ tāmbūlaṃ bhājanāni ca |
brāhmaṇāya pradadyāt tu yadyat pretasya vāñchitam ||
verses.translation: Sandals, an umbrella, a seat, betel, and vessels — whatever was desired by the departed — all should be given to a brahmin.

verses.hindi:
वृषोत्सर्गं ततः कुर्यात् एकादशदिनेऽवशम् ।
शिववर्णो वृषो ग्राह्यः लक्षणैरुपलक्षितः ॥
verses.transliteration:
vṛṣotsargaṃ tataḥ kuryāt ekādaśa-dine'vaśam |
śiva-varṇo vṛṣo grāhyaḥ lakṣaṇair upalakṣitaḥ ||
verses.translation: Then the bull release ceremony must be performed on the eleventh day. A bull of auspicious color, marked with proper characteristics, should be obtained.

verses.hindi:
वृषं सालङ्कृतं कृत्वा गन्धपुष्पैः समर्चयेत् ।
शिवमन्त्रेण संपूज्य ततो विसर्जयेत् ततः ॥
verses.transliteration:
vṛṣaṃ sālaṅkṛtaṃ kṛtvā gandha-puṣpaiḥ samarchayet |
śiva-mantreṇa saṃpūjya tato visarjayet tataḥ ||
verses.translation: Having adorned the bull, one should worship it with fragrant flowers. Having honored it with the Shiva mantra, one should then release it.

verses.hindi:
वृषोत्सर्गं विना तार्क्ष्य प्रेतस्य न विमुक्तता ।
धर्मराजपुरे घोरे वैतरण्यां तु बध्यते ॥
verses.transliteration:
vṛṣotsargaṃ vinā tārkṣya pretasya na vimuktatā |
dharma-rāja-pure ghore vaitaraṇyāṃ tu badhyate ||
verses.translation: Without the bull release, O Tarkhsya, there is no liberation for the departed. In the terrible city of the King of Dharma, at the river Vaitarani, the soul is bound.

verses.hindi:
वैतरणीं महानदीं तर्तुं शक्नोति वृषभात् ।
वृषस्य पुच्छं संगृह्य तरति दुस्तरां नदीम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
vaitaraṇīṃ mahā-nadīṃ tartuṃ śaknoti vṛṣabhāt |
vṛṣasya pucchaṃ saṃgṛhya tarati dustarāṃ nadīm ||
verses.translation: By means of the bull, the departed can cross the great river Vaitarani. Grasping the tail of the bull, the soul crosses that impassable river.

verses.hindi:
एकादशदिने विप्रान् भोजयेद् एकविंशतिम् ।
षड्रसोपेतमन्नं च पायसं च प्रदापयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
ekādaśa-dine viprān bhojayet ekaviṃśatim |
ṣaḍ-rasopetam annaṃ ca pāyasaṃ ca pradāpayet ||
verses.translation: On the eleventh day, twenty-one brahmins should be fed. Food prepared with six flavors and rice pudding should be offered.

verses.hindi:
ब्राह्मणान् सम्पूज्य विधिवत् दक्षिणां च प्रदापयेत् ।
वस्त्रभूषणगोदानैः तोषयेत् द्विजसत्तमान् ॥
verses.transliteration:
brāhmaṇān sampūjya vidhivat dakṣiṇāṃ ca pradāpayet |
vastra-bhūṣaṇa-go-dānaiḥ toṣayet dvija-sattamān ||
verses.translation: Having worshiped the brahmins according to the rites, one should give them dakshina (fee). With gifts of garments, ornaments, and cows, one should gratify the best among the twice-born.

verses.hindi:
पिण्डदानं विशेषेण एकादशदिने भवेत् ।
त्रयोदश पिण्डान् दद्यात् सतिलांश्च सदर्भकान् ॥
verses.transliteration:
piṇḍa-dānaṃ viśeṣeṇa ekādaśa-dine bhavet |
trayodaśa piṇḍān dadyāt sa-tilāṃśca sa-darbhakān ||
verses.translation: The pinda offering on the eleventh day is especially significant. Thirteen pindas should be given, mixed with sesame and placed on darbha grass.

verses.hindi:
प्रथमं पिण्डं विष्णवे द्वितीयं शिवसंज्ञकम् ।
तृतीयं यमदेवाय चतुर्थं प्रेतभोजनम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
prathamaṃ piṇḍaṃ viṣṇave dvitīyaṃ śiva-saṃjñakam |
tṛtīyaṃ yama-devāya caturthaṃ preta-bhojanam ||
verses.translation: The first pinda is for Vishnu, the second is designated for Shiva, the third is for the lord Yama, and the fourth is food for the departed soul.

verses.hindi:
दानधर्मं प्रकुर्वीत एकादशदिने सदा ।
घृतधेनुं गुडधेनुं तिलधेनुं जलाशयम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
dāna-dharmaṃ prakurvīta ekādaśa-dine sadā |
ghṛta-dhenuṃ guḍa-dhenuṃ tila-dhenuṃ jalāśayam ||
verses.translation: Charitable acts should always be performed on the eleventh day: the gift of a cow made of ghee, a cow of jaggery, a cow of sesame, and a water reservoir.

verses.hindi:
रत्नदानं सुवर्णं च रजतं ताम्रमेव च ।
धान्यं भूमिं तथा गावः दद्यात् प्रेतविमुक्तये ॥
verses.transliteration:
ratna-dānaṃ suvarṇaṃ ca rajataṃ tāmram eva ca |
dhānyaṃ bhūmiṃ tathā gāvaḥ dadyāt preta-vimuktaye ||
verses.translation: Gems, gold, silver, copper, grain, land, and cows should be given for the liberation of the departed soul.

verses.hindi:
विष्णोर्नामसहस्रं तु पठेत् तत्र समाहितः ।
प्रेतमुद्दिश्य विप्रेभ्यो दद्यात् पुस्तकमुत्तमम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
viṣṇor nāma-sahasraṃ tu paṭhet tatra samāhitaḥ |
pretam uddiśya viprebhyo dadyāt pustakam uttamam ||
verses.translation: The thousand names of Vishnu should be recited there with concentration. On behalf of the departed, an excellent sacred text should be given to the brahmins.

verses.hindi:
गरुडपुराणं श्रावयेत् ब्राह्मणान् भक्तिसंयुतः ।
पुराणश्रवणात् प्रेतो मुक्तो भवति बन्धनात् ॥
verses.transliteration:
garuḍa-purāṇaṃ śrāvayet brāhmaṇān bhakti-saṃyutaḥ |
purāṇa-śravaṇāt preto mukto bhavati bandhanāt ||
verses.translation: The Garuda Purana should be recited for the brahmins to hear, with devotion. By hearing the Purana, the departed is freed from bondage.

verses.hindi:
स्नापनं प्रेतरूपस्य कारयेत् तद्दिने बुधः ।
पञ्चगव्येन शुद्ध्यर्थं शान्तिकर्म च कारयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
snāpanaṃ preta-rūpasya kārayet tad-dine budhaḥ |
pañca-gavyena śuddhyarthaṃ śānti-karma ca kārayet ||
verses.translation: The wise one should cause the bathing of the effigy of the departed on that day. For purification, panchagavya (five cow products) should be used, and peace ceremonies should be performed.

verses.hindi:
अशौचं दशमे नष्टं एकादशदिने शुचिः ।
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन एकादशदिने क्रियाम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
aśaucaṃ daśame naṣṭaṃ ekādaśa-dine śuciḥ |
tasmāt sarva-prayatnena ekādaśa-dine kriyām ||
verses.translation: The ritual impurity ends on the tenth day; on the eleventh day one becomes pure. Therefore, with every effort, the rites of the eleventh day should be performed.

verses.hindi:
एकादशे दिने यस्तु विधिवत् कर्म कारयेत् ।
तस्य प्रेतः सुसन्तुष्टो दिव्यलोकं प्रगच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
ekādaśe dine yastu vidhivat karma kārayet |
tasya pretaḥ susantuṣṭo divya-lokaṃ pragacchati ||
verses.translation: He who performs the rites on the eleventh day according to the proper procedure — his departed one, fully satisfied, proceeds to the divine realm.

verses.hindi:
ये त्वेकादशकर्माणि न कुर्वन्ति विमोहिताः ।
तेषां प्रेताः पिशाचत्वं यान्ति कल्पशतानि च ॥
verses.transliteration:
ye tvekādaśa-karmāṇi na kurvanti vimohitāḥ |
teṣāṃ pretāḥ piśācatvaṃ yānti kalpa-śatāni ca ||
verses.translation: Those who, being deluded, do not perform the eleventh-day rites — their departed ones become pishacha (malevolent spirits) for hundreds of cosmic ages.

verses.hindi:
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन एकादशदिने शुभे ।
कर्म कुर्यात् यथोक्तं हि प्रेतमुक्तिप्रदायकम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
tasmāt sarva-prayatnena ekādaśa-dine śubhe |
karma kuryāt yathoktaṃ hi preta-mukti-pradāyakam ||
verses.translation: Therefore, with every effort, on the auspicious eleventh day, one should perform the rites as prescribed, for they are the bestowers of liberation to the departed.
